methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals deliberate community-driven AI literacy distribution in Richmond, creating the kind of peer network infrastructure that could spread deep capability beyond NoVA's tech hubs if successfully implemented and replicated.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - MSN
signals.signal_reading: Pressure on Richmond's young workforce suggests AI adoption is concentrating in metro employment markets, creating capability demands that may deepen the urban-rural divide unless training infrastructure distributes regionally.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents grassroots capacity-building in historically underserved regions, testing whether distributed deep learning can take root outside traditional tech corridors through localized networks.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Platform-delivered AI tools for home searches in Hampton Roads exemplify shallow but distributed adoption—business efficiency gains without building local AI development capacity, reinforcing consumption over creation outside NoVA.

signals.title: Virginia’s AI Data Center Gravy Train Shows Signs of Slowing - Bloomberg Law News
signals.signal_reading: Slowing data center growth in NoVA signals concentration limits being reached in the state's AI infrastructure hub, raising questions about whether capability will redistribute to other regions or simply stagnate in existing pockets.
